segments.text: Why do notebooks have margins? Most
people think it's for your teacher to
leave comments, but they're completely
wrong. This red line was actually
designed as a shield against a
disgusting enemy from the past. It's a
design solution to a problem that we
don't have anymore. But it saved the
world's most important information from
being eaten. Back in the day, paper was
incredibly expensive and precious. But
libraries had a massive problem. Rats.
These rodents love snacking on the salty
edges of paper. If you wrote your notes
right to the edge, the rats would
literally eat your data. You would wake
up and your work would be gone. So
designers created a buffer zone. They
added a margin so that the rats would
only eat the blank space, leaving the
actual writing safe in the middle. The
margin was literally a sacrificial piece
of paper. But why does it still exist?
Take a guess in the comments.
